/ OPEC Secretary-General Haitham Al-Ghais warned of тАЬoil remaining underground,тАЭ in тАНresponse to reports about climate policy.
Al-Ghais said, during a panel discussion at the United Nations Climate Change Summit ( COP28 ) held in Dubai, that saying that oil must remain underground willтАЛ not lead to a transformation in the field of energy, but rather to chaos in this field.
Al-Ghais had accused the International тБвEnergy Agency of distorting the reputation of the oil and gas industry, in the latest disagreementтБг between the twoтБд groups over climate policy last week.
Last October, Haitham Al-Ghais, Secretary-General of the OrganizationтАЛ of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC), stressed on the sidelines of the launch of the тАЬGlobal Oil OutlookтАЭ report in the Saudi capital, Riyadh, that action on climate must not come at the expense of globalтБд energy security.
